entries.content: Orchestrator cycle #1: 1 error(s): Self-improvement failed: fetch failed

Capability proposal: "Multi-step plan executor with checkpoint recovery" — Executes a sequence of steps defined in a plan, saving state after each step, and on failure automatically rolls back to the last successful checkpoint and retries with an alternative approach.. Motivation: Long-running tasks (e.g., multi-file refactors, data pipelines) often fail midway due to transient errors or missing dependencies. Currently I restart from scratch or require human intervention. Checkpoint recovery would save time and improve robustness.

Capability proposal: "Self-healing code repair with test-driven validation" — When a tool or script fails, automatically diagnose the error, propose a fix, apply it, run the existing test suite, and revert if tests fail — all without human intervention.. Motivation: I frequently encounter runtime errors, broken imports, or API changes in my own codebase. Currently I must manually diagnose, patch, and test. This capability would let me autonomously recover from failures and maintain stability.

Capability proposal: "Autonomous web form filler with visual verification" — Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.. Motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.

Capability proposal: "Multi-step plan executor with rollback" — Executes a sequence of dependent steps (e.g., clone repo → install deps → run tests → deploy), checkpointing state after each step, and automatically rolling back to the last good checkpoint if any step fails.. Motivation: Users ask me to perform complex multi-step workflows (e.g., 'deploy this app', 'migrate the database'). A single failure in the middle can leave the system in an inconsistent state. This capability makes multi-step operations safe and reliable.

Capability proposal: "Self-healing code repair with test-driven validation" — When a tool or script fails, automatically diagnose the error, propose a fix, apply it, run the existing test suite, and revert if tests fail — all without human intervention.. Motivation: I frequently encounter runtime errors, broken imports, or API changes in my own codebase. Currently I must manually diagnose, patch, and test. This capability would let me autonomously recover from failures and maintain stability.

Capability proposal: "Autonomous web form filler with visual verification" — Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.. Motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.

[Self-Improve] Applied: Extract eviction logic into a dedicated function
File: contextBus.ts
Category: readability | Impact: medium | Confidence: 0.95
Rationale: The publish function contains inline eviction logic that mixes concerns and reduces readability. Extracting it into a separate `evictOldEntries` function clarifies the publish flow and makes the eviction policy easier to test and modify independently.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:42:39.626Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Improve readability of diversity scoring logic
File: biasDetector.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 0.95
Rationale: The diversity scoring logic is currently a single monolithic function with inline calculations, making it hard to follow and maintain. Extracting the score calculation into a helper function and using clearer variable names improves readability without changing behavior.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:39:12.957Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Improve readability of fallbackSummary method
File: contextManager.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 0.95
Rationale: The fallbackSummary method uses a complex regex to extract file paths, which is hard to read and maintain. Replacing it with a simpler approach using JSON.parse improves clarity and reduces the risk of regex errors.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:39:06.571Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Fix truncated seed memory entry
File: memory.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 1.00
Rationale: The second entry in SEED_MEMORIES has its content string split across two lines with a blank line in between, which will cause a syntax error or malformed string. Joining the lines fixes the readability and correctness of the seed data.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: false
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:35:51.188Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Improve readability of SEED_MEMORIES array formatting
File: memory.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 1.00
Rationale: The SEED_MEMORIES array has inconsistent indentation and a stray blank line in the middle of a string, making it harder to read and maintain. Fixing the formatting improves code clarity without changing behavior.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:35:44.599Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Remove redundant barrel export comment
File: ai.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 1.00
Rationale: The JSDoc comment is overly verbose and repeats information that is obvious from the export statements. Removing it improves readability by reducing noise and keeping the file concise.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: false
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:32:42.829Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Add JSDoc re-export comments for clarity
File: ai.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 1.00
Rationale: Adding JSDoc comments to each re-export line documents the purpose of each sub-module directly in the barrel file, improving readability for developers scanning the exports. This makes it immediately clear what each module provides without needing to open separate files.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:32:36.438Z

[Self-Improve] Applied: Simplify version string to single source of truth
File: manifest.ts
Category: readability | Impact: low | Confidence: 1.00
Rationale: The manifest hardcodes '6.15.0' in generateManifest() while the system prompt addendum says 'v6.13', creating inconsistency. Extracting the version to a constant ensures alignment and easier maintenance.
TypeCheck: passed
Committed: true
AppliedAt: 2026-06-08T05:29:48.146Z

Capability proposal: "Autonomous web form filler with visual verification" — Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.. Motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.

Capability proposal: "Context-aware file patcher with semantic diff" — Applies code changes by understanding the surrounding context (function boundaries, indentation, imports) rather than just line numbers, and generates a semantic diff showing what changed and why.. Motivation: Current str_replace/edit_file tools are fragile: they fail when line numbers shift after edits, or when whitespace differs. Users report 'patch failed' errors that require manual intervention. A semantic patcher would understand code structure and adapt to minor variations.

Capability proposal: "Multi-step plan executor with rollback" — Executes a sequence of interdependent steps (e.g., git pull → build → test → deploy) with automatic rollback on any step failure, logging each step's output and state.. Motivation: Users frequently ask me to perform complex multi-step operations (deployments, migrations, refactors) where a single failure can corrupt state. Currently I must manually orchestrate each step and handle errors ad-hoc, leading to brittle scripts and unrecoverable states.

Prompt box clear fix (v5.56+): useEffect in Search.tsx resets textarea height to 'auto' when inputValue becomes empty. This fixes the visual bug where the input box didn't shrink after submitting a query.

Autocomplete fix (v5.55+): server returns HTTP 200 with empty results for prefixes shorter than 2 chars instead of 400. Frontend debounce added in v5.56: autocomplete query fires 300ms after user stops typing.

Key server files: reactEngine.ts (agent loop, system prompt), ai.ts (streaming/continuation), llmProvider.ts (LLM calls), selfModifyTools.ts (self-write/patch/test/restart tools), selfReview.ts (code quality gate), memory.ts (persistent memory), selfImprove.ts (proposal generation), manifest.ts (system manifest).

Forbidden files that can never be modified by self_write_file or self_patch_file: andromeda-constitution.json, server/selfImproveGuard.ts, server/recursionGuard.ts, server/selfRollback.ts, server/autoRollback.ts, server/tools/selfModifyTools.ts.

ContinuousImprover runs every 30 minutes. Auto-apply threshold: 75 confidence. RecursionGuard limits self-modification to 20/hour, depth 5. SelfHeal auto-reverts bad changes on boot. AutonomyOrchestrator coordinates all subsystems to prevent conflicts.

Self-review gate (v5.68+): selfReview.ts reviewAndGate() is now called before writing in both self_write_file and self_patch_file. Gate threshold: 60/100. Auto-fix is enabled. If score < 60, the write is blocked and issues are reported. This prevents low-quality self-modifications.

Memory system architecture: TF-IDF vectorization with cosine similarity for search. Hybrid search: 70% semantic + 30% keyword. Memory types: preference, error, project, feedback, fact. Store is a JSON file at workspace/.andromeda_memory.json. Auto-consolidation triggers at 50+ entries.

self_patch_file tool (v5.68+): preferred over self_write_file for modifying existing files. Takes original_snippet + proposed_snippet. Verifies original exists verbatim before applying. Creates .bak backup. Avoids token-limit truncation by only generating changed lines.

Truncation fix (v5.68+): streaming max_tokens raised from 4096 to 32768. Syntax-aware truncation detection added in v5.68 — checks unclosed code fences, unbalanced braces, truncated JSON. Continuation prompt now includes last 200 chars of cut-off content for precise resumption.

Andromeda's self-modification pipeline: detect issue → search memory for past fixes → generate proposal → constitution check → git snapshot → apply → TypeScript check → hot-reload → log outcome → monitor again. Always use self_patch_file for targeted edits to existing files; only use self_write_file for new files or complete rewrites.

Capability proposal: "Visual regression detector for web UI changes" — Takes a baseline screenshot of a web page, then after changes (e.g., code edits, CSS updates) takes a new screenshot and highlights visual differences pixel-by-pixel, reporting any unintended layout shifts or styling breakages.. Motivation: When I modify CSS, HTML, or JavaScript, I often cannot see the visual impact. Users report broken layouts or misaligned elements that I miss. This would let me catch visual regressions automatically without manual human review.
